definitions.definition: The process of gradual and cumulative changes in the characteristics of a species over a period of time, resulting in the formation of new species.
A group of living organisms that can interbreed and produce fertile offspring, and are reproductively isolated from other groups.
The differences in characteristics among individuals of a species, which provide the raw material for evolution.
The genetic makeup of an individual, including the combination of genes it possesses.
The physical and behavioral characteristics of an individual, resulting from the interaction of its genotype and the environment.
Structures in different species that have a common evolutionary origin, but have different functions.
Structures in an organism that have lost their original function through evolution, but are still present.
A characteristic or trait that has evolved to help an organism survive and reproduce in its environment.
The process by which individuals with favorable traits are more likely to survive and reproduce, passing those traits on to their offspring.
The process of selecting and breeding individuals with desired traits to produce a new variety or breed.
The process by which a new species forms from an existing one, often as a result of geographical isolation or other barriers to gene flow.
The theory that evolution occurs gradually, over a long period of time, through the accumulation of small changes.
The theory that evolution occurs suddenly, in response to sudden changes in the environment.
A record of the history of life on Earth, preserved in the form of fossils of ancient organisms.
The study of the distribution of organisms on Earth, and how it relates to their evolution and adaptation to different environments.
A method of estimating the rate of molecular evolution, based on the accumulation of mutations in DNA or proteins over time.
The study of the evolutionary relationships among organisms, including the history of their divergence and speciation.
A method of classifying organisms based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
An organism that gave rise to a group of related organisms through evolution.
